verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is synthesized as a large latent complex (LLC): the mature TGF-β1 dimer remains non-covalently associated with the latency-associated peptide (LAP/LTBP scaffold) and is secreted or deposited into the ECM. Critically, LRRC32/GARP and LRRC33/NRROS act as disulfide-tethered cell-surface TM receptors that covalently anchor the LTBP-independent small latent complex (LAP-TGF-β1) to the outer leaflet of regulatory T cells, platelets, macrophages, and other immune/stromal cells. This constitutes wash-resistant, covalent tethering to a TM partner — not transient receptor binding. The surface-tethered (GARP-bound or NRRC33-bound) LAP-TGF-β1 pool is the target of clinical-stage antibodies (e.g., anti-GARP/TGF-β1 bispecifics). The protein reaches the outer leaflet only in cell types expressing GARP or LRRC33, making this contextual/stable_surface_attachment rather than constitutive.
